powerbi-modeling-mcp.comment: Choose ONE of the configurations below based on your installation method

_option_1_npx.description: Power BI MCP Server (NPX) - Auto-downloads latest version

_option_2_manual.description: Power BI MCP Server (Manual) - Update path to your installation

powerbi-modeling-mcp.note: VS Code Extension users don't need this file - configuration is automatic

setup: 2. Enable XMLA Endpoint in Power BI Desktop (Options → Preview Features)
3. Copy this file to ~/.vscode/mcp-settings.json or workspace .vscode/mcp-settings.json
5. Open your Power BI model in Power BI Desktop
troubleshooting: - Verify Power BI Desktop is running
- Verify MCP server appears in GitHub Copilot tools
- For NPX: Ensure Node.js is installed (node --version)
- For Manual: Verify executable path is correct

POWERBI_MODE.note: Use 'local' for Power BI Desktop (recommended for optimization)

AUTO_CONNECT.description: Automatically discover and connect to running Power BI Desktop

BENCHMARK_WARM_ITERATIONS.description: Number of warm cache benchmark iterations
BENCHMARK_WARM_ITERATIONS.note: Warm cache iterations test performance after data is loaded into memory

BENCHMARK_COLD_ITERATIONS.description: Number of cold cache benchmark iterations
BENCHMARK_COLD_ITERATIONS.note: Cold cache iterations test performance with cleared cache (realistic first-load scenario)

ENABLE_TEMP_MEASURES.description: Allow creation of temporary measures for testing

CLEANUP_ON_EXIT.description: Automatically delete temporary measures on completion

CACHE_CLEAR_METHOD.description: How to clear cache between benchmark runs
CACHE_CLEAR_METHOD.note: Some Power BI versions require 'manual' mode

authentication.description: For Power BI Service (not needed for local)

performance_tuning.note: Adjust based on your hardware and model size
